# omada_ip_group (Resource)

Manages an IP/Port group on the Omada Controller. IP groups are used as source or destination in firewall ACL rules for port-based filtering. Requires a gateway device adopted into the site.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ip_list` (Attributes List) List of IP address and port combinations. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ip_list))
- `name` (String) The name of the IP group.
- `site_id` (String) The ID of the site this resource belongs to.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the IP group.
- `type` (Number) The group type. Use 1 for IP/Port group.

<a id="nestedatt--ip_list"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ip_list`

Required:

- `ip` (String) IP address or CIDR subnet (e.g., '192.168.1.100' or '192.168.1.0/24').

Optional:

- `port_list` (List of String) List of port numbers or ranges as strings (e.g., '80', '7000-7100').
